_comment: Copy to data/people.json and fill in. data/people.json is gitignored: it names real people.
_directory: The full path to the folder that holds this person's documents. THE FOLDER IS THE PATIENT -- we walk the exact path you give, never a folder matched by name, because names drift (a rename, a trailing space, a duplicate copy) and a document filed against the wrong person is the worst failure this system can have. Folders can live anywhere; there is no shared root. A folder belonging to no listed person is simply never looked at -- add a new family member by adding them here.
_sex: Used to pick a reference range when a lab printed none. Male and female normals genuinely differ (creatinine, uric acid, HDL, haemoglobin).
_child: Adult reference ranges are WRONG for children -- paediatric normals differ hugely by age. For a child we only ever use the range the LAB printed on the report, which is age-appropriate. If no range is printed, we flag nothing: an absent flag is honest, a wrong one is dangerous.
_aliases: Optional. Whatever YOU call this person -- 'dad', 'appa', 'the boss'. Purely your own vocabulary, so the agent can answer 'when did my dad have this'. Relationships live here, in your config, and never in the code: every family names itself differently.
_deceased: Optional. A deceased person's record is history, not something to maintain: no reconciliation cards, no follow-up reminders, no review nags. The records themselves stay exactly as they are.
_born: Optional, for children. A document dated before this date cannot be theirs.
